titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 575

p.seg: David, king of the Scots, to Gervase Ridel, sheriff of Roxburgh, greeting.

I enjoin you that the land of the monks of Durham which Cospatric of Dunbar gave [?upon his father's death] be placed in respite until I shall have come to that province. No-one shall demand of the monks nor of Cospatric any work nor service, but the monks shall hold that land well and in peace, and quit within their boundary. And you are not to allow anyone to do injustice but act thereupon just as you would do for my demesne.
